_comment: Single source of truth for the game version shown on the main menu. Newest release first. When you make a player-facing change, PREPEND a new entry (bump the version, set today's date, write a short summary + change bullets). See CLAUDE.md 'Version / changelog' for the convention.

releases.summary: The AI now plays 11th edition properly — stratagems, faction rules, mission play — and explains every decision in the Game Log.
changes: The AI's reasoning is now shown in detail in the left Game Log panel: each decision appears as a card with the options it weighed — including moves, charges and stratagems it considered and REJECTED, with the why. Click 'considerations' on a card to expand them.

The AI now uses the full 11th-edition core stratagem set: Insane Bravery before critical battle-shock tests, Epic Challenge to strike attached characters, Crushing Impact after Monster/Vehicle charges, Fire Overwatch, Smokescreen (fixed — it never fired before), Heroic Intervention with the 11e Leap to Defend / Into the Fray modes, Counteroffensive, Rapid Ingress and Command Re-rolls.

The AI now uses its detachment stratagems: War Horde Orks throw 'Ere We Go, Mob Rule, Grab and Bash and Unbridled Carnage; Shield Host Custodes use Archeotech Munitions, Avenge the Fallen, Multipotentiality, Vigilance Eternal and defensive reactions.

The AI answers faction rule windows it previously ignored or stalled on: Martial Ka'tah stances, Plant the Waaagh! Banner, Da Kaptin, Grot Orderly, Fix Dat Armour Up, Unleash the Lions and Psychic Veil.

The AI now reads its own 11th-edition primary mission card and plays to it — pushing the enemy home objective, the board centre, or kills, whichever its card actually pays for — and pursues all seven new secondary cards (Beacon, Outflank, Plunder, Forward Position, Burden of Trust, Centre Ground, A Grievous Blow).

11th-edition rules fixes in the AI's math: 2-inch engagement range in charge planning, cover as -1 to hit, post-roll charge target re-selection, Ordered Retreat vs Desperate Escape fall-back modes, and a fixed battle-shock re-roll that was evaluating the wrong way around.
Normal difficulty (the default) now uses stratagems and survival judgement — the AI should be noticeably more challenging out of the box. Easy is unchanged for learning the game.

releases.summary: Reworked the main menu around 11th-edition Force Dispositions.
changes: Moved Army Selection above Mission Settings on the main menu.
The Force Disposition dropdowns now sit at the top of Mission Settings, since they drive everything else in 11th edition.
Primary Mission is now shown read-only — it is derived from each player's Force Disposition pairing (each player can play a different card).
Deployment Zone is now shown read-only — it follows from the chosen terrain variant.
Terrain Layout now lists just the 3 official variants for the current disposition matchup.

releases.summary: Show the game version and recent changes on the main menu.
changes: Added a version badge (number + date) beneath the main-menu title so it is easy to tell which build is running.
Added a 'What's New' panel to the main menu summarizing the most recent changes.
